author: gurukamath
content:
<@968526635735326741> Is it safe to assume that [this PR](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11645) is the general direction that EIP-2780 is moving in and that we are just waiting for the final numbers from the benchmarking? If that is how it is, I can get moving on updating the EELS implementation and tests to align with this.
IIRC, there are no other design points that need clarification

author: draganrakita
content:
I am reading the EIP and can we simplify edge cases:
* Remove self-transfer case
* Remove precompile case
These are really not necessary or important and just make EIP more complex.

author: gurukamath
content: Good point. They made sense from the initial thought process behind the EIP. That you pay for whatever work has to be done. But they are probably too niche to carve out an special rule in the spec.

author: draganrakita
content: self-transfer does nothing, it is basically a troll call. While precompile call can be used as a cheaper way to "burn" eth but this does not make sense to stipend burning of eth 😄

author: misilva73
content:
Yes, I think this is the general direction. However, two caveats there:
1. We will likely not have the cost of an ETH transfer to an existing EOA go above the 21k. We are currently collecting benchmarks to check the base performance of these transfers and we will likely adjust the numbers based on the results.
2. Once the BAL optimizations are more stable, we will see if the performance of accessing EOA is significantly lower than accessing contracts. At the moment, their are not, but this may change. If it does change, we may want to bring back the CODE NO_CODE costs

author: misilva73
content: I was trying to cover as many cases as possible. I think is is usually better to have more info the EIP than less info

author: draganrakita
content: But this is not about more info, it introduces additional checks that are not necesarry. Now in txpool we need to know what precompile addresses are, it complicates things.

author: misilva73
content: Ah, so you are saying that self trasfers and precompiles should pay the same as a transfer to a EOA?

author: draganrakita
content: Yeah, would just remove those cases

author: misilva73
content: Ah, got it. <@316280621783580673> , would you be ok with this simplification?

content: Precompiles I don't care about; sending ETH to a precompile is just silly 🙂

content: But to self you are already updating balance; so is net 0

author: ben_a_adams
content: Also already don't emit Eth transfer event for this case; so have the branch?

content: Could be sending 50bits of data to an inbox with your blob 🤔
